name: Kailasa
description: Kailasa, also known as Mount Kailash, is a sacred mountain in the Tibetan Himalayas, considered the abode of Lord Shiva in Hindu mythology. It is revered as a holy site by Hindus, Buddhists, Jains, and followers of the Bon religion. The mountain's distinct pyramid shape and perpetual snow-capped peak contribute to its aura of divinity. Pilgrims undertake a challenging circumambulation (parikrama) of the mountain as an act of devotion, believing it cleanses sins and brings good fortune. Despite numerous attempts, no one has ever successfully climbed Kailasa out of respect for its sacred nature and the belief that disturbing its sanctity would bring misfortune. The Mansarovar Lake and Rakshastal Lake, located near Kailasa, are also considered sacred and integral to the spiritual significance of the region. The mountain represents the axis mundi, the center of the universe, and serves as a powerful symbol of spiritual enlightenment and liberation. Its remote location and challenging terrain further enhance its mystique and draw devotees seeking inner peace and connection with the divine. The mountain is often depicted in Hindu art and literature as the idyllic home of Shiva and Parvati, a place of serene beauty and profound spiritual energy. It embodies the ideals of renunciation, devotion, and the ultimate union of the individual soul with the divine.
subtitle: Abode of Shiva; Axis Mundi

keyMyths.title: Shiva's Abode
keyMyths.description: Kailasa is described as the home of Lord Shiva and his consort Parvati, where they reside in eternal bliss and meditation. It is a place of peace, power, and spiritual significance.

keyMyths.title: Ravana's Attempt
keyMyths.description: Ravana, the demon king, attempted to uproot Kailasa to display his power, but Shiva pressed his toe, crushing Ravana beneath the mountain. Ravana then sang hymns to Shiva for forgiveness.

sources.author: Swami Sivananda
sources.description: Details the significance of Shiva and his association with Mount Kailasa.
sources.text: Details the significance of Shiva and his association with Mount Kailasa.

sources.author: Alain Danielou
sources.description: Provides context on the role of Kailasa within the wider Hindu pantheon.
